mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an impending economic contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower future cash flows and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during historical recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between consumer credit expansion and housing starts indicates a structural shift in aggregate demand composition, where rising credit coupled with falling housing construction predicts a near-term equity rally as liquidity flows into financial assets rather than real estate.
failure_condition: The cross-product of RSAFS and HOUST shows no predictive power for SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal flips direction during periods of high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, elevated policy rates combined with sticky inflation compress equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are at multi-year highs, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during the 2022-2023 tightening phase.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous growth in real GDP per capita drives corporate revenue expansion, which mechanically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day SPX returns drops below 0.10 over a 10-year sample, or quarterly GDP revisions consistently precede unexplained equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below its long-term trend signals supply-side bottlenecks resolving into demand weakness, prompting a negative S&P 500 forward return signal over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO crosses below 100, or the signal generates a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of strong employment growth and rising unemployment indicates a cooling labor market where job creation slows faster than layoffs rise, creating a lagged negative pressure on equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns with a t-statistic above 1.96 over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the signal direction reverses during the 2020 pandemic shock.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing market activity signals a liquidity-driven demand surge that temporarily inflates equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as investor sentiment improves.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
